text: The surgeon begins the procedure by making an incision along the anovaginal line, gaining access to the avascular rectovaginal septum.
The surgeon identifies and mobilizes the two stumps of the interrupted external sphincter. The stumps are overlapped and sutured using interrupted PROLINE sutures.
The surgeon performs a suture on the anterior anal wall beneath the sphincter to close the defect, using precise stitching techniques.

The surgeon performs a plastic reconstruction using a pedicle pudendal fasciocutaneous flap. This vertical vulvoperineal flap is visibly vascularized by the terminal branches of the internal pudendal artery.

The surgeon measures and delimits a skin flap on the genitocrural fold, with the middle edge positioned lateral to the labia majora. An incision is made through the skin, subcutaneous tissue, and adductor muscle fascia. The flap is lifted, rotated ninety degrees, and fixed with interrupted Vicryl 3-0 sutures to cover the repaired external sphincter.
